purpose: Write structured decision records for gates, policy checks, and workflow approvals so later review can explain why a system allowed, denied, or rerouted behavior.

endpoints.description: Persist a structured decision record with actor, policy, result, and evidence fields.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Persist structured decision logs for policy and workflow review.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use after a meaningful allow, deny, gate, reroute, or approval decision has been computed and should be reviewable later.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use as a general-purpose application logger, metrics sink, or high-volume debug stream.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Compute a decision. 2) Call /audit/decisions with decision metadata. 3) Store the returned log id alongside the business object if needed.

failure_modes.condition: Decision record is missing core metadata or contains unserializable evidence
failure_modes.recovery: Normalize the evidence payload and provide required decision metadata before retrying

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Accept a typed decision envelope, normalize any evidence payload to JSON-safe form, and persist immutable log entries with generated ids.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Audit records lose value when timestamps or decision types are free-form and inconsistent across callers, so centralize field naming and required metadata.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Write append-only records and treat updates or redactions as separate audit events when needed.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not reuse debug logging infrastructure as the decision audit layer.
